retry_reason: kref_get_unless_zero fails only when the refcount is zero (object is being freed/destroyed). Retrying will not revive the object, so it is pointless.
contract_summary: Acquires a reference on a batadv_orig_ifinfo structure that matches the given outgoing interface. The function iterates under RCU read lock and uses kref_get_unless_zero to safely increment the refcount only if the object is still alive. Returns NULL if no matching entry is found or if the refcount reached zero. The caller must check the return value and must release the reference with batadv_orig_ifinfo_put when the object is no longer needed. The RCU lock is held only during lookup, so the caller must not depend on RCU protection after the function returns.

judge.reason: Primary purpose is to conditionally acquire a reference (kref_get_unless_zero) on a matching orig_ifinfo entry. The only non-refcount logic is the list iteration and interface comparison, which are minimal and serve only to locate the correct object.

check.reason: kref_get_unless_zero is called on &tmp->refcount, and tmp is assigned to orig_ifinfo which is returned. The access path from orig_ifinfo (return variable) through refcount (field in batadv_orig_ifinfo) -> refcount (field in kref) -> refs (field in refcount_t) matches callee info. Type chain and segment count are consistent.
